purpose: Quantifies the cost of last-mile disinflation in terms of output loss. Answers: how much growth must be sacrificed to bring core PCE from ~2.5-3% to the 2% target?
components: Sacrifice ratio of 4-5x for last-mile disinflation
Fiscal floor preventing sufficient output gap opening
Sargent-Wallace fiscal dominance as the macro constraint
Supply-chain normalization gains exhausted — remaining inflation is demand-driven
current_assessment: Core PCE has stalled at ~2.5-3% after rapid decline from 5.6%. The fiscal deficit prevents the output gap from opening enough for the 4-5x sacrifice ratio to operate. This creates a policy trap where the Fed cannot achieve its inflation target without fiscal cooperation that is politically unavailable. 5Y5Y breakevens at 2.3% suggest partial de-anchoring.
limitations: Sacrifice ratio estimate is imprecise and regime-dependent
A favorable supply shock could provide exit without demand destruction
Orphanides/van Norden output gap measurement error of ±1.0-2.0pp makes real-time assessment difficult
